summary: Consensus Cloud Solutions supports the proposed deregulatory actions in HTI-5, emphasizing the reduction of certification burdens to foster innovation and improve interoperability. They highlight the benefits of removing outdated requirements like C-CDA creation and easing audit log and EHI export mandates.
key_arguments: Reducing certification requirements lowers development costs and accelerates innovation.
Removing outdated standards like C-CDA creation aligns with current interoperability practices.
Easing audit log and EHI export requirements reduces unnecessary burdens without compromising security.
Simplifying USCDI data class requirements allows more flexible data exchange.
recommendations: Proceed with removing the C-CDA creation certification criterion.
Reduce audit log and EHI export requirements as proposed.
Maintain focus on burden reduction to encourage health IT innovation.
notable_quote: Reducing certification requirements lowers development costs and accelerates innovation.
